content.Text:
[Thu 2026-04-23 14:59 PDT] Add a "Movie Rental Negotiator" feature to this ASP.NET MVC Vidly app. This is an autonomous negotiation engine where customers can "negotiate" rental terms and the system autonomously decides on deals.

## Architecture Pattern
Follow the EXACT same patterns as existing features (look at TasteEvolutionController.cs, TasteDnaController.cs, etc.):
- Controller in `Vidly/Controllers/NegotiatorController.cs`
- Service in `Vidly/Services/NegotiationService.cs`  
- View model(s) in `Vidly/Models/` (NegotiationModels.cs)
- Views in `Vidly/Views/Negotiator/` (Index.cshtml)
- Add nav link in `Vidly/Views/Shared/_NavBar.cshtml`

## Core Models (Vidly/Models/NegotiationModels.cs)
```csharp
// NegotiationStrategy enum: Loyal, Casual, Premium, FirstTime, Bulk
// NegotiationOffer: MovieId, MovieName, OriginalRate, OfferedRate, DiscountPct, ExtendedDays, BonusPerks (list of strings), ExpiresAt, Confidence (0-1)
// NegotiationRound: RoundNumber, CustomerArgument (string), SystemResponse (string), OfferAdjustment (decimal), NewOffer (NegotiationOffer)
// NegotiationSession: CustomerId, CustomerName, Rounds (list), Strategy, LoyaltyScore (0-100), InitialOffer, FinalOffer, Status (Negotiating/Accepted/Rejected/Expired), ProactiveInsights (list of strings)
// NegotiationViewModel: Customers list, SelectedCustomerId, Session, ErrorMessage, AvailableMovies
```

## Service (Vidly/Services/NegotiationService.cs)
Depends on IMovieRepository, IRentalRepository, ICustomerRepository.

Key logic:
1. **ClassifyCustomer(customerId)** - Analyze rental history to determine NegotiationStrategy. Count total rentals, check for late returns, genre diversity. Loyal = 20+ rentals with <10% late. Premium = high avg daily rate. FirstTime = <3 rentals. Casual = rest.
2. **CalculateLoyaltyScore(customerId)** - 0-100 score based on: rental count (max 30pts), on-time return rate (max 25pts), genre diversity (max 15pts), total spending (max 20pts), membership length in months (max 10pts).
3. **GenerateInitialOffer(customerId, movieId)** - Create initial NegotiationOffer based on strategy and loyalty. Base discount: Loyal=15%, Premium=10%, Casual=5%, FirstTime=8%, Bulk=20%. Modify by loyalty score. Add perks like "Free extra day", "No late fee guarantee", "Priority reservation next time".
4. **Negotiate(session, customerArgument)** - Process a negotiation round. The system "considers" the argument and adjusts. Max 5 rounds. Each round, the system can improve by 1-3% more discount or add a perk. Arguments containing words like "loyal", "years", "always" get +2% bonus. "Competitor", "cancel", "expensive" get +1.5% urgency bonus. System confidence decreases with each round. After round 3, system starts saying "this is my best offer".
5. **GetProactiveInsights(session)** - Generate 3-5 insights like "Based on your history, you watch mostly Action movies - we've added a free Action rental perk", "Your on-time return rate of 95% qualifies you for our reliability bonus", "Suggesting a 3-movie bundle could save you 25% more".

## Controller (Vidly/Controllers/NegotiatorController.cs)
Standard MVC pattern with constructor DI like TasteEvolutionController.
- `GET Index(?customerId, ?movieId)` - Show negotiation UI with customer/movie selection
- `POST Negotiate(customerId, movieId, argument)` - Process a negotiation round (store session in TempData as JSON)
- `POST Accept(customerId)` - Accept the current offer
- `POST Reject(customerId)` - Walk away

## View (Vidly/Views/Negotiator/Index.cshtml)
Full interactive UI with:
- Customer and movie selection dropdowns
- "Start Negotiation" button
- Chat-like negotiation interface showing rounds with customer messages on right, system on left
- Current offer card showing: movie, original rate, offered rate, discount %, extended days, perks
- Loyalty score gauge (circular progress)
- Strategy badge
- Proactive insights panel
- Accept/Reject/Counter buttons
- "Make your case" text input for counter arguments
- Nice styling consistent with the app (use Bootstrap classes if available, otherwise inline CSS)

## Nav Integration
Add to _NavBar.cshtml - look at how other items are added and follow the same pattern. Add under a "Smart Tools" or similar dropdown if one exists, or alongside existing nav items. Icon: 🤝

## Important Notes
- Use `using System;`, `using System.Collections.Generic;`, `using System.Linq;`, `using System.Web.Mvc;` etc.
- Use `Vidly.Models`, `Vidly.Repositories`, `Vidly.Services` namespaces
- Constructor takes repos, creates service
- Default parameterless constructor uses InMemory repos (same pattern as TasteEvolutionController)
- Do NOT add any NuGet packages
- Do NOT modify existing files except _NavBar.cshtml (add nav link only)
- After implementing, verify build with: `dotnet build Vidly.sln --no-restore -q 2>&1` (if that fails try `dotnet build --no-restore -q`)
I’m inspecting the existing MVC feature patterns first so the negotiator feature matches the app’s controller, service, model, view, and navbar conventions exactly.

ToolUse.name: Run rg -n "class TasteEvolutionController|class TasteDnaController|_NavBar|class .*Service|TempData" Vidly -S

content.Text: I’ve confirmed the app already has many feature-specific MVC slices. Next I’m reading the reference controllers, service patterns, repositories, and the navbar so the new feature fits the existing wiring instead of inventing a new convention.

I have the controller and navbar pattern now. I’m reading the core domain models and a couple of session-driven controllers so I can match the repository APIs and TempData/JSON handling instead of guessing field names.
